questions: Should existing shots in the menu show ALL shots that contain ANY of the selected generation IDs, or only shots that contain ALL selected generation IDs? (e.g., 3 clips selected, Shot A has 2 of them — does Shot A appear?)
For the gallery SelectionContextMenu: the selected gallery IDs may be generation IDs directly, or they may be image IDs that need mapping. Which is it?

Should we deduplicate — i.e., if the user just created a shot from the menu (shown via createdShot local state), should that same shot also appear in the existing shots list, or should we filter it out to avoid duplication?

success_criteria.criterion: Right-clicking a clip shows existing shots that contain the clip's generation ID, with Jump to Shot and Generate Video buttons for each
Create Shot and Generate Video buttons still appear below existing shots
After creating a shot via context menu, right-clicking the same clip again shows the newly created shot in the existing shots section
Multi-clip selection shows all shots that contain any of the selected generation IDs
Works in both ClipAction context menu (timeline) and SelectionContextMenu (gallery)
clearSelection() no longer runs after shot creation, so shot actions remain available on subsequent right-clicks
All existing video editor and SelectionContextMenu tests pass
No unnecessary re-renders — existing shots lookup uses useMemo with proper dependencies
Menu items for existing shots are visually distinct from creation actions (separator or subtle label)
Each modified file stays under ~300 lines
Full manual smoke test of all 6 scenarios in Step 7

assumptions: Existing shots should appear if they contain ANY of the selected generation IDs (partial overlap is sufficient) — this is the most useful behavior since it surfaces all related shots.

The clearSelection() call after shot creation should be removed entirely rather than deferred — the user can dismiss the selection by clicking elsewhere, and keeping the selection lets them right-click again to see the newly created shot.
No new database queries are needed — the client-side shots from useShots() already include images with generation_id, so filtering is cheap.
The SelectionContextMenu in the gallery will need similar treatment but the gallery uses generation IDs directly via the GallerySelectionContext.

The createdShot local state in the menu should coexist with the existing shots list — after creating a shot, the menu shows both the 'Jump to' for the just-created shot AND lists it in existing shots on subsequent right-clicks.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
